title: Add scrape_interval as a config option
summary: The charm exposes per-target prometheus options (metrics_path, scheme, params, tls_config_*, basic_auth) but has no scrape_interval option, so operators cannot override the default 1m interval for non-juju targets (the issue gives haproxy@15s as the motivating case). The fix is to add scrape_interval to charmcraft.yaml config.options and wire it into the rendered job exactly like metrics_path/scheme are at src/charm.py:107-112.
why_good_first_issue: Self-contained, additive change in two well-understood spots (config schema + one loop in _scrape_jobs). The existing test test_scrape_job_has_specified_labels (tests/unit/test_charm.py:78) is a copy-paste template for a new unit test asserting the option lands in the job. No architecture knowledge required.
affected_files: charmcraft.yaml (config.options - currently no scrape_interval); src/charm.py:107-112 (the metrics_path/scheme option loop in _scrape_jobs)
repro_steps: On VM: added a temp test mirroring tests/unit/test_charm.py that calls harness.update_config({'targets':'foo:1234','scrape_interval':'15s'}) and inspects the rendered scrape_jobs. Ran via tox -e unit / pytest.
repro_result: REPRODUCED (unit). The Harness (which loads the real charmcraft.yaml config schema) raised: ValueError: unknown config option: 'scrape_interval' — proving the option does not exist in the charm's config and thus cannot be set or propagated into the scrape job. Observed: no scrape_interval option / it never reaches the job. Expected-after-fix: setting scrape_interval='15s' yields job['scrape_interval']=='15s'. Baseline suite: 11 passed.
fix_hint: Add to charmcraft.yaml config.options: scrape_interval (type: string, description). In _scrape_jobs, add 'scrape_interval' to the option tuple at src/charm.py:107-110 so 'if value := self.model.config.get(option): job.update({option: value})' picks it up. Add a unit test asserting the rendered job contains the interval.
notes: Enhancement (not a bug) but unusually clean and beginner-sized; labels Type: Enhancement, Status: Triage. Still open & valid. ops resolved 2.x; also note src/charm.py:16 uses deprecated 'from ops.main import main'.

title: Yaml validation failed, but no error shown in Juju status
summary: When prometheus rejects the charm's scrape config (e.g. unquoted label values, issue #52), prometheus writes a scrape_job_errors message into the relation databag, but prometheus-scrape-target shows ActiveStatus and never surfaces the failure. _update_prometheus_jobs sets ActiveStatus() as soon as the locally-built jobs list is non-empty (src/charm.py:91-92) and never observes/reads back the remote 'event'/'scrape_job_errors' field. Operators only discover the problem by noticing the target missing from the Prometheus UI.
why_good_first_issue: Clear, well-described user-facing bug with a known trigger and a focused fix area (status reporting in one handler). Good for an intermediate first-timer; slightly above 'trivial' because it requires reading the remote relation databag and adding an observer.
affected_files: src/charm.py:81-94 (_update_prometheus_jobs sets ActiveStatus unconditionally when jobs is non-empty); relation 'metrics-endpoint' databag field scrape_job_errors written by prometheus (per issue logs)
repro_steps: Repro plan (needs live deploy): 1) juju deploy prometheus-k8s and prometheus-scrape-target-k8s. 2) Configure a target with an unquoted label value that fails cos-tool validate-config, e.g. labels="dns_name:k8s-cluster-ai-1.maas". 3) juju relate the two. 4) Observe: scrape-target unit shows 'active' while 'juju show-unit prometheus-scrape-target/0' relation application-data contains event: {"scrape_job_errors": "...validate-config... non-zero exit status 1"} and the target is absent from the Prometheus UI.
repro_result: LIVE-REPRODUCED 2026-06-16: prometheus-k8s + scrape-target 2/stable; labels=1bad:value -> scrape-target ACTIVE (no error) while promtool check config FAILED: '1bad is not a valid label name'. Charm sets Active from local jobs only, never reads scrape_job_errors (charm.py:91-92). || needs-live-deploy (flagged for orchestrator). Code-level confirmation: src/charm.py:91-92 sets ActiveStatus() purely on local jobs being non-empty; grep shows the charm never reads 'scrape_job_errors' (no occurrence in src/charm.py), so a remote validation error cannot affect unit status. Cannot reproduce purely at unit tier because the error originates from prometheus' cos-tool writing back into the relation.
fix_hint: Observe self.on[metrics-endpoint].relation_changed and read the remote app/unit databag for 'scrape_job_errors' (or the 'event' JSON); if present, set BlockedStatus('Prometheus rejected scrape config, see debug-logs') instead of ActiveStatus. Closely related to #52 (label quoting) and #54 (stale data after failed validation).
notes: Same root area as #52/#54. Cannot be unit-reproduced without a real prometheus; flagged integration. Baseline unit suite passes (11 passed).
